title: Phonon promoted charge density wave in topological kagome metal ScV6Sn6
type1_summary: Both files are Nature source-data PDFs and are most consistent with cleaned, figure-oriented supplementary data intended for replotting rather than raw instrument outputs.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies the CDW in the kagome metal ScV6Sn6 using STM, ARPES, Raman scattering, and DFT. The authors find a √3×√3×3 CDW with an in-plane √3×√3 R30° reconstruction, topological surface states and van Hove singularities near EF, and Raman signatures of strong electron-phonon coupling, arguing that lattice effects promote the CDW while VHS-related electronic structure may contribute to the in-plane ordering vector.

figures.description: Crystal structure of ScV6Sn6 in the normal state, showing the layered unit cell and kagome lattice.
Temperature-dependent in-plane resistivity showing a transition near 92 K.
Temperature-dependent specific heat capacity showing a transition near 92 K.
STM topograph and Fourier transform of the Sn2 termination in the normal state, showing atomic lattice and no CDW superlattice peaks.
STM topograph and Fourier transform in the CDW phase, showing √3×√3 R30° reconstruction and CDW peaks.
Bulk and surface Brillouin zone schematic with high-symmetry points.
Schematic of in-plane folding from pristine to √3×√3 CDW Brillouin zone.
Schematic of the kagome surface termination used for ARPES/XPS comparison.
Sn 4d XPS core-level spectrum and integrated EDC used to identify the surface termination.
Fermi surface map on the kagome termination.
Polarization- and photon-energy-dependent ARPES band dispersions along Γ-M and Γ-K on the kagome termination.
Schematic of the ScSn3 termination.
Sn 4d XPS core-level spectrum and integrated EDC for the ScSn3 termination.
Fermi surface maps and ARPES band dispersions on the ScSn3 termination.
Zoomed ARPES Fermi surface map around Γ on the ScSn3 termination showing circular and hexagonal sheets.
ARPES band dispersion along Γ-M highlighting topological surface states.
Stacked constant-energy ARPES maps around K showing Dirac cones.
Second-derivative ARPES dispersion along Γ-K-M-Γ showing Dirac cones and van Hove singularities.
DFT-calculated band structure along Γ-K-M-Γ with Dirac cones and VHSs marked.
Schematic of the surface Brillouin zone and cut directions used for ARPES analysis.
ARPES cuts across K-M showing the saddle-point nature of VHS1.
ARPES cuts across K-M showing VHS2.
ARPES cuts perpendicular to Γ-K showing VHS3 near Λ.
Schematic of nesting vectors for 2×2 and √3×√3 CDW scenarios.
Temperature-dependent ARPES band dispersions below and above TCDW showing negligible reconstruction.
Temperature-dependent Raman colormap showing phonon modes, a broad two-phonon feature, and emergent CDW modes.
Representative Raman spectra below and above TCDW with mode assignments.
Calculated Raman mode frequencies near Γ.
Temperature dependence of the A3 Raman mode frequency and linewidth.
DFT phonon dispersion along high-symmetry paths and K-H-K, showing imaginary modes and the two-phonon energy window.
Reference distortion pattern for 2×2×1 CDW in CsV3Sb5.
Illustration of the acoustic phonon mode and the √3×√3×3 CDW distortion pattern in ScV6Sn6.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports direct experimental measurements including STM topographs on different surface terminations, transport resistivity, specific heat capacity, ARPES spectra and Fermi-surface maps with varying photon energy/polarization, Sn 4d XPS core-level spectra for termination identification, and temperature-dependent Raman spectra/colormaps.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper includes processed/derived plots such as Fourier transforms of STM images, second-derivative ARPES band maps, stacked constant-energy maps, fit-extracted Raman peak frequency and linewidth versus temperature, and DFT-calculated electronic and phonon band structures.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The provided text does not include a data availability statement or explicit repository/file description; only methods and figure captions are visible.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as experimental measurements from STM, transport, heat capacity, ARPES, XPS, and Raman instruments. The text does not specify file formats, repositories, or whether raw files are deposited.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include Fourier transforms of STM topographs, ARPES intensity maps and second-derivative spectra, temperature-dependent Raman peak fits (frequency and linewidth), and DFT-calculated band/phonon dispersions. The paper also discusses integrated EDCs for XPS core levels.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly present in the form of plotted spectra, maps, and fit curves, but the text does not state that source-data files are separately provided.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: mixed / figure-based in the manuscript; external organization unknown
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The manuscript shows many plots and derived curves, but the provided text does not confirm availability of underlying numerical source data, raw files, or scripts needed for direct replotting.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes multiple surface terminations and two sample batches with different TCDW values (92 K and 80 K). It uses both raw experimental observables and derived analyses (FFT, second derivatives, fits, DFT). The text suggests figure-level data exist, but there is no explicit statement about deposited raw datasets or source-data files in the excerpt.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct measurements from STM, resistivity, heat capacity, ARPES, XPS, and Raman scattering, all of which generate raw experimental data.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The figures include processed/derived outputs such as Fourier transforms, second-derivative ARPES maps, fit-extracted Raman parameters, and DFT calculations, which are typical source-data/processed figure data.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The manuscript clearly contains both raw experimental measurements and processed figure data, but the excerpt does not specify whether both are deposited as downloadable files.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: No explicit data availability section is included in the provided text; relevant phrases are in Methods and figure captions describing STM topographs, ARPES spectra, Raman response maps, and DFT calculations.
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to equate plotted figures with raw data. The manuscript strongly implies processed figure data and raw measurements, but the excerpt does not confirm repository deposition, file formats, or scripts.
